title: Statement Regarding the Policy on Counterparties for Market Operations and Reverse Repurchase Counterparties

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York issued an Operating Policy Statement on April 25, 2023, updating its Policy on Counterparties for Market Operations and Reverse Repo Counterparties. The updates clarify that broader policy goals including financial stability and bank safety and soundness are now explicitly considered when reviewing counterparties. For RRP counterparties, the policy now explicitly requires that accessing reverse repo operations be a natural extension of an existing business model, and that counterparties not be organized specifically for the purpose of accessing RRP operations.
key_points: NY Fed updated Policy on Counterparties for Market Operations to explicitly consider broader policy goals including financial stability and bank safety and soundness
RRP counterparty expectations updated to require that accessing reverse repo operations be a natural extension of existing business models
Counterparties organized specifically for accessing RRP operations are explicitly prohibited under the updated policy
SEC registered 2a-7 funds organized for a single beneficial owner are generally ineligible for reverse repo operations
Funds serving as cash management vehicles for multiple funds or investment entities in an investment fund complex remain generally eligible
The NY Fed characterizes these changes as clarifications of existing practices rather than substantive policy changes
why_it_matters: This policy update defines the structural framework for who can participate in Federal Reserve market operations, specifically the reverse repo facility. By clarifying eligibility criteria and explicitly prohibiting entities organized solely to access the RRP, the NY Fed is shaping the composition of participants in a key short-term funding market and monetary policy implementation channel.
macro_context: The RRP facility serves as a key tool in the Federal Reserve's monetary policy implementation, allowing eligible counterparties to place funds with the Fed overnight. This update follows a period of elevated RRP usage and appears designed to prevent regulatory arbitrage structures.
market_structure_context: The policy defines counterparty eligibility for Fed market operations and the overnight reverse repo facility, directly affecting the structure and participants in short-term funding markets. The exclusion of single-owner 2a-7 funds targets a specific type of entity that had been accessing RRP operations.
